text.title: competitive-intelligence-methods Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for competitive-intelligence-methods as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Treating the competitive analysis as a standing document rather than a dated snapshot.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A feature matrix without a capture date is an undated fact claim that degrades silently. Readers cannot tell if they are looking at last week's reality or last year's.

failure-mode.description: Running the feature matrix without synonym merging across competitor feature names.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The same capability under different names produces multiple rows with conflicting Y/N/P values, making aggregation impossible and gap identification unreliable.

failure-mode.description: Treating listed prices on enterprise tiers as ACVs for B2B pricing analysis.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "Talk to Sales" tiers are anchors, not ACVs — actual discounts can be 50-80% off list. Tag these rows with confidence: low, and cross-reference with G2 reviewer-reported pricing.

failure-mode.description: Marketing-page parroting: LLM paraphrases the competitor's homepage tagline as "positioning" without checking third-party sources.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force evidence_urls length ≥2 with at least one third-party source. Homepage taglines are marketing copy, not observable positioning in buyer perception.

failure-mode.description: Numeric hallucination: funding totals, ARR, headcount fabricated without source_url.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reject any numeric field without source_url. "Unknown" is the correct output when API access is absent, not a plausible invention.

failure-mode.description: Optimistic gap recommendations: LLM default helpful tone recommends every gap as an opportunity without adversarial review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The gap-validator critic with a forced skip budget ≥40% is non-negotiable. Without it, the gap list becomes a feature-bloat backlog.

failure-mode.description: Token blowup from passing full G2 review HTML to the matrix filler.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Never paste full review HTML into the matrix-filler. The review-miner must distill to top-5 complaints per competitor first, then pass the distillation.

failure-mode.description: Human-in-the-loop checkpoint missing: gap list becomes spec input without founder review of the kill-list rationale.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents under-skip (helpful bias), founders over-skip (loss aversion) — the diff between their lists is the real shortlist. Skip the checkpoint and you get either feature bloat or missed opportunities.
